summary: Researchers from Imperial College London, National Taiwan University, Brookhaven National Laboratory, Chung Yuan Christian University, JIJ, NVIDIA, ORCA Computing, and University of Oxford introduce Photonic Quantum-Enhanced Knowledge Distillation, a hybrid quantum-classical framework leveraging programmable photonic circuits for model compression in machine learning. The approach uses the intrinsic stochasticity of photonic quantum processors to create a compact conditional feature map, reducing trainable parameters in convolutional neural networks. Validated on datasets including MNIST, Fashion-MNIST, and CIFAR-10, the study shows parameter-count reductions while retaining model performance, and robustness to noise and hardware perturbations. This work demonstrates the practical potential for photonic quantum devices to enhance classical machine learning models via structured randomness in real-world noisy environments.
